title: Curriculum-standards mapping for the four families
context: Teachers and curriculum coordinators want to know which learning standards a manipulative supports before adopting it. A curriculum-standards mapping document helps educators identify which of the four core families (fractions, counters, number lines, base-ten blocks) align with standards they teach. The mapping must be descriptive and non-prescriptive -- it identifies alignment, not mandates, and must remain accessible to educators worldwide by mapping to at least one widely used international or US framework without endorsing any particular curriculum approach.
objective: Research and write a descriptive curriculum-standards mapping document for all four core manipulative families, mapping each to at least one common standards framework, reviewed by a math educator.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the four manipulative families is mapped to at least one widely used standards framework (e.g. US Common Core State Standards for Mathematics, or an equivalent international framework).
Mapping is descriptive and non-prescriptive: it identifies which standards each manipulative supports, not which curriculum to follow.
Sources are cited; framework citations include version/year.
Reviewed and approved by a qualified math educator; reviewer recorded.
Published CC-BY-4.0; written to be accessible to educators internationally (avoids US-only framing where possible).
resources: tasks/math-manip-data-002.json -- the four core families this document maps
Common Core State Standards for Mathematics (CCSS-M)
NCTM Principles and Standards for School Mathematics
output: A curriculum-standards mapping document (docs/curriculum-standards-mapping.md), CC-BY-4.0, with per-family standards alignment and math-educator sign-off.
